title: Dental Check-up & Cleaning
shortDescription: Comprehensive oral exam with gentle professional cleaning.
overview: A thorough clinical examination paired with professional scaling and polishing to keep gums healthy and catch concerns early.
benefits: Early detection of cavities and gum issues
procedure: Full mouth examination and digital imaging if needed
Scaling, polishing, and oral hygiene coaching
recovery: Resume normal activities immediately. Mild sensitivity may last a day.

faqs.question: How often should I get a cleaning?
faqs.answer: Most patients benefit from a professional cleaning every 6 months. We’ll tailor the interval to your gum health.

title: Root Canal Treatment
shortDescription: Pain-focused endodontic care to save your natural tooth.
overview: Precision root canal therapy using modern techniques to remove infection, relieve pain, and preserve your natural tooth.
procedure: Cleaning, shaping, and sealing of canals
recovery: Mild tenderness for 1–3 days. Soft foods recommended initially.

faqs.question: Is a root canal painful?
faqs.answer: With modern anesthesia and gentle technique, most patients report relief rather than pain during the procedure.

title: Teeth Whitening
shortDescription: Clinically supervised whitening for a brighter, natural smile.
overview: Professional whitening tailored to your enamel health — delivering noticeable brightness without aggressive over-the-counter guesswork.
recovery: Temporary sensitivity possible for 24–48 hours. Avoid staining foods briefly.
suitableFor: Stains from coffee, tea, or aging

faqs.question: How long do results last?
faqs.answer: Results typically last several months to a year depending on diet and maintenance. Touch-ups keep your shade bright.

title: Dental Implants
shortDescription: Stable, natural-feeling tooth replacement for missing teeth.
overview: Titanium implant solutions planned with digital diagnostics for strength, aesthetics, and long-term oral health.
benefits: Looks and feels like a natural tooth
recovery: Soft diet for a few days. Full integration typically over several weeks.
duration: Planning + surgical visit; crown after healing

faqs.question: Am I a candidate for implants?
faqs.answer: Most healthy adults with adequate bone are candidates. We’ll assess gum health, bone volume, and medical history first.

title: Dental Crowns
shortDescription: Custom ceramic crowns that restore strength and beauty.
overview: Tooth-colored crowns crafted to protect weakened teeth while blending seamlessly with your smile.
recovery: Slight adjustment period for bite comfort. Normal eating within a day for most patients.

faqs.question: How long do crowns last?
faqs.answer: With good oral hygiene, quality ceramic crowns often last 10–15 years or longer.

title: Smile Makeover
shortDescription: Personalized aesthetic planning for a confident, natural smile.
overview: A curated combination of whitening, bonding, aligners, or veneers — designed around your facial features and goals.
procedure: Smile analysis and digital preview discussion
recovery: Depends on selected treatments; most aesthetic steps have minimal downtime.
duration: Custom — from a single visit to a staged plan

faqs.question: Will my smile look artificial?
faqs.answer: No. We design for harmony with your face — brightness, shape, and proportion that look naturally yours.

title: Braces & Clear Aligners
shortDescription: Straighten teeth discreetly with guided orthodontic care.
overview: Metal braces and clear aligner options planned for comfort, predictability, and a confident finish.
recovery: Mild pressure for a few days after adjustments or new trays.

faqs.question: Are clear aligners right for me?
faqs.answer: Many mild-to-moderate cases are excellent aligner candidates. We’ll confirm after a clinical assessment.

title: Pediatric Dentistry
shortDescription: Gentle dental care that helps children feel safe and confident.
overview: Age-appropriate check-ups, preventive care, and kind chairside manner for growing smiles.
procedure: Parent guidance on diet and brushing
recovery: None — children return to normal activities immediately.

faqs.question: When should my child first visit?
faqs.answer: Ideally by age one or within six months of the first tooth. Early visits make future care easier.
